---
title: "Groundhog Day 2026 Prediction"
---

> "When Chekhov saw the long winter, he saw a winter bleak and dark and bereft of hope. Yet we know that winter is just another step in the cycle of life." — *Groundhog Day* (1993)

Real Bayesian methods applied to the least serious dataset available: over a century of groundhogs guessing at the weather.

Every February, dozens of groundhogs across the US and Canada "predict" whether winter will run long (saw shadow) or end early (no shadow). The [groundhog-day.com API](https://groundhog-day.com/api/v1) has recorded these predictions back to 1886. This treats that record as a genuine binomial time series: is the shadow rate actually 50/50, does it drift over time or by region, and which groundhogs are consistently more "shadow-prone" than others, once you account for the fact that some of them have one data point and Punxsutawney Phil has 127.

- [Shadow EDA](notebooks/01_shadow_eda.ipynb) — frequentist exploration: overall and yearly shadow rates, binomial and chi-square tests, groundhog-type breakdown.
- [Bayesian hierarchical model](notebooks/02_bayesian_hierarchical.ipynb) — the real analysis: partial pooling by groundhog, a groundhog x decade model, posterior ability rankings, and a map of the registry.

Source and reproduction steps: [github.com/korentomas/groundhog_day_2026_pred](https://github.com/korentomas/groundhog_day_2026_pred).
